scope_contract: Per AC-12.1 / Section 5.4 rule 3: only violations newly introduced in this diff are emitted; every finding tagged introduced_in_diff:bool. close.md uses tag per AC-2.6 (b).

naming_violations.reason: New shell verifier file does not follow the existing hooks/tests/test_* test-file pattern used by tracked tests (test_allowlist_consolidation.py, test_cp_checkin.py, test_commit_strip_dotfile_paths.py) and by sibling new file test_push_sentinel_abort.sh. Underscore-prefix is Python private-module convention, atypical for shell tests.
naming_violations.recommendation: If durable, rename to test_ac1_verify.sh; if task-private, remove or archive after cycle closes.

naming_violations.reason: Underscore-prefix breaks hooks/tests/test_* convention. Functions as a top-level test driver; should follow standard test naming if retained.
naming_violations.recommendation: Rename to test_final_sweep.sh or remove after cycle closes.

unreferenced_scripts.reason: New task-specific verifier; verified by grep against hooks/, commands/, agents/, policies/ that nothing functional invokes this file. _final_sweep.sh (sibling driver) does NOT call _ac*_verify.sh — it embeds its own equivalent checks inline and only invokes test_push_sentinel_abort.sh at line 56. Only references are auto-generated INDEX.md entries.
unreferenced_scripts.recommendation: Either wire _final_sweep.sh to invoke _ac1_verify.sh, or remove/archive the standalone file post-cycle. Note: out-of-scope contextual references in docs/dev/completion-20260519-211515.md exist but are not in the changed-files diff and therefore cannot establish non-orphan status under --changed-files audit scope.

unreferenced_scripts.reason: Same as _ac1_verify.sh — no functional invocation in canonical dirs; _final_sweep.sh embeds equivalent checks inline rather than calling this file.
Same as _ac1_verify.sh — no functional invocation; _final_sweep.sh duplicates the check logic inline.

permission_observations.reason: The seven new shell scripts are mode 644 (not executable). Sibling new file test_push_sentinel_abort.sh is 755. Functionally tolerated where callers use `bash <script>`, but inconsistent with the executable-shell convention.
permission_observations.recommendation: If these files are retained, chmod +x to match the canonical 755 convention; if they are removed/archived (per naming_violations + unreferenced_scripts findings above) this becomes moot.

summary.estimated_space_saved: negligible (small shell scripts; 7 files totaling ~20KB if archived)

verdict_input_for_close.close_recommendation: non-blocking — all findings are minor naming/wiring/permission anomalies in newly-introduced task-private verifier scripts. None block CLOSE.

out_of_scope_pre_existing_dirt_observed_: Mixed 644/755 permission convention across the broader hooks/ Python dir (pattern is pre-existing inconsistency, not introduced by this diff)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex provided 4 constructive PROPOSED_FIX entries: (1) tighten naming_violations wording to specify rename target or archive disposition; (2) correct permission_observation rationale — _final_sweep.sh does NOT invoke _ac*_verify.sh files (confirmed by direct grep, only line 56 invokes test_push_sentinel_abort.sh); (3) MISSED orphan finding — _ac*_verify.sh are unreferenced by any caller in the diff; _final_sweep.sh embeds duplicate inline checks; (4) no-orphans rationale was using out-of-scope evidence (completion-*.md, qa-report-*.json) that are not in the changed-files list and cannot establish non-orphan status under --changed-files audit scope.

findings.issue: naming_violations wording lacked concrete rename target / disposition guidance
findings.proposed_fix: Rephrase to specify rename to test_ac*_verify.sh and test_final_sweep.sh if durable, else remove/archive.
findings.rationale: Improved actionability of each naming_violations entry; recommendation field now states explicit rename target.

findings.issue: permission_observation rationale incorrectly implied _final_sweep.sh invokes _ac*_verify.sh via bash
findings.proposed_fix: Correct rationale: _final_sweep.sh does NOT invoke them; they are functionally orphaned within the diff. Demote to advisory consistency observation.
findings.rationale: Verified by grep on hooks/tests/_final_sweep.sh — only line 56 invokes test_push_sentinel_abort.sh; _ac*_verify.sh files have zero invocations in the script. Rationale corrected.

findings.issue: Missing orphan finding: _ac*_verify.sh are unreferenced verifier scripts
findings.proposed_fix: Add unreferenced_scripts entries flagging each _ac*_verify.sh with introduced_in_diff:true and advisory recommendation (wire into _final_sweep.sh or remove/archive).
findings.rationale: Verified by grep across hooks/, commands/, agents/, policies/ — only INDEX.md auto-generated mentions exist. Six new unreferenced_scripts findings added, all introduced_in_diff:true, all advisory non-blocking.

findings.issue: Initial no-orphans rationale relied on out-of-scope completion-*.md and qa-report-*.json references
findings.proposed_fix: Restrict orphan-status rationale to listed changed files only. External docs may be noted as contextual but cannot establish non-orphan status under --changed-files scope.
findings.rationale: Recommendation field in each unreferenced_scripts finding now explicitly notes the scope contract and labels external docs as contextual only.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 4 codex items applied: (1) naming_violations recommendations tightened with rename targets; (2) permission_observation rationale corrected to remove false claim about _final_sweep.sh invocation chain; (3) six new unreferenced_scripts findings added covering _ac{1,3,5,6,9,10}_verify.sh, each tagged introduced_in_diff:true and advisory; (4) orphan rationale scope-restricted with explicit note about --changed-files contract.
